titleStmt.title: SII 19.260: original edition by G.V. Srinivasa Rao, Dines Chandra Sircar and G.S. Gai
No. 260. (A.R. No. 1 of 1920.) TIRUMAḺAVĀḌI, UDAIYARPALAYAM TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT. ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, VAIDYANĀTHA TEMPLE.

summary.p: This records the gift of a silver vessel weighing 5 palam or 77(3/4) kaḻañju for offering betel-nut to god Tirumaḻavāḍi-Uḍaiyār, by queen Nakkaṉ Tillai-Aḻagiyār Śōḻamādēviyār. Pañchavan-Mādēvi the queen of Rājarāja was also known as Nakkaṉ Tillaiyaḻagiyār (A.R. No. 47 of 1928). This is probably assignable to Uttama-Chōḷa. But immediately below and in continuation of this is engraved No. 2 of 1920 which is a record of his pradecessor Madiraikoṇḍa Rājakēsari (Sundara-Chōḷa).

revisionDesc.change: Further conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)
Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti śrī . kopparakecaripa
nmarkku yāṇṭu 10Āvatu tiruma
ḻuvāṭi Uṭaiyārkku nampirāṭṭiyār na
kkan tillaiaḻakiyārāna coḻa
māteviyār vaiytta piṅkāḷa
m Eḻupatteḻukaḻañcey mukkā
l Itu Aṭaikkāy Amutu cey
ya vaiyccatu Itu vaiyakattārkka
l Aimpalam Itu veḷḷiyāl
itu panmāheśvararakṣai .
Digital edition of SII 19.260 by converted to DHARMA conventions by Emmanuel Francis.
